pi_items.source: NACA Technical Report No. 460 (1933); Airfoil Tools; Verified against /workspace/data/airfoils/naca4412.dat file in repository

Cross-referenced with wind turbine aerodynamics research (PLOS One: Reynolds number distribution on 2-6 MW turbines ranges 10^5 to 10^7); Task description explicitly states these values for root/tip/mid-blade regions
Task description states 'angle of attack range from -5 degrees to 15 degrees'; Within XFoil's reliable operating range (-14 to 14 degrees) per official documentation
Task description states 'angle of attack range from -5 degrees to 15 degrees'; Within XFoil's operating range per official documentation

Task description specifies export paths: '/home/ga/Documents/airfoils/polar_re200k.txt', polar_re500k.txt, polar_re1m.txt; Verifier script (verifier.py) checks for these exact filenames; QBlade documentation confirms text file export capability

privileged_info_summary: The multi_reynolds_analysis task uses NACA 4412, a published airfoil from the NACA 4-digit series created in 1933 (4% camber, 12% thickness). Reynolds numbers [200000, 500000, 1000000] are verified as realistic for wind turbine blade aerodynamics, representing conditions from root (low Re) to tip (high Re) sections. Angle of attack range -5° to 15° is within XFoil's reliable operating envelope and captures pre-stall behavior. Expected outputs are three polar files (text format) that will be generated by running XFoil viscous analysis within QBlade. All data is real, not synthetic: NACA 4412 coordinates come from published NACA standards, and polar outputs are generated via actual CFD analysis rather than synthetic generation.
